channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: India's EV Push Faces Economic Strain from West Asia Oil Tensions - Whalesbook
item.description: India's EV Push Faces Economic Strain from West Asia Oil Tensions Whalesbook

item.title: Radio Station Owner Spanish Broadcasting Files for Bankruptcy
item.description: Radio station owner Spanish Broadcasting System Inc. filed for bankruptcy on Monday after announcing plans to hand ownership to noteholders in exchange for restructuring the debt.

item.title: Education Budget will grow every year, says Telangana CM Revanth Reddy
item.description: Allocations to the sector would be gradually increased to 15% of the annual Budget, says Reddy, also the Education Minister, while launching the ‘Exclusive Education Week’ celebrations in Hyderabad; also unveils ₹1,700-crore infrastructure works for integrated school complexes, additional classrooms and pre-primary sections in government schools

item.title: Modi’s Frugality Warning Flags Twin Shock for India - Bloomberg.com
item.description: Modi’s Frugality Warning Flags Twin Shock for India Bloomberg.com

item.title: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli West Bank settlers, Hamas leaders
item.description: The long-awaited agreement comes after Hungary's new government dropped the country's veto.

item.title: Bank of America delays Fed rate cuts to 2027 amid persistent inflation - Crypto Briefing
item.description: Bank of America delays Fed rate cuts to 2027 amid persistent inflation Crypto Briefing

item.title: Low-Cost Airlines ‘Ripe’ For Mergers, Deutsche Bank Analyst Says
item.description: The US airline industry is primed for a new round of mergers as low-cost carriers are squeezed by the oil-price spike, according to Deutsche Bank analyst Michael Linenberg.

item.title: ONGC Mulls New ₹200 Cr Fund To Back Energy & AI Startups
item.description: State-run oil and gas company Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) is planning to launch a new ₹200 Cr fund…

item.title: Telus CEO of 26 Years Urges Successor to Take Risks, Trust Team
item.description: Telus Corp. Chief Executive Officer Darren Entwistle shared parting words of wisdom for his replacement, shortly before he leaves the job he’s occupied for 26 years.

item.title: Natural gas mineral firm WhiteHawk reveals sevenfold revenue surge in US IPO filing
item.description: WhiteHawk, a leading natural gas royalty firm, is taking the stock market plunge with its initial public offering. This comes on the heels of an astonishing 615% revenue spike in 2025, mirroring the current energy market's thrumming heart as oil prices rise. With vast natural gas holdings over millions of acres, WhiteHawk plans to reward its investors with consistent dividends.

item.title: Carlyle BDC Cuts Dividend Even As It Flags Better Credit Market
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Carlyle Group Inc. cut the value of its assets in the first quarter and lowered its dividend, while reporting that it originated more loan deals.

item.title: Omar Abdullah’s ‘liquor consumption religion-centric’ comment sparks debate
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the region's liquor policy, stating shops are for those whose religion permits consumption. His comments come amid a 100-day anti-drug campaign, with calls to extend it to liquor. Abdullah highlighted his government's efforts to prevent youth from consuming alcohol, including not opening new shops.

item.title: Natural Gas, WTI Oil, Brent Oil Forecasts – Oil Gains 4% As Trump Says Ceasefire Is On “Life Support” - FXEmpire
item.description: Natural Gas, WTI Oil, Brent Oil Forecasts – Oil Gains 4% As Trump Says Ceasefire Is On “Life Support” FXEmpire

item.title: EU says OpenAI offers to open access to cybersecurity model, Anthropic not there yet
item.description: The European Commission has expressed enthusiasm for OpenAI's generous offer to grant open access to its advanced cybersecurity capabilities, a move set to fortify security standards and enhance public safety across Europe. Conversely, rival entity Anthropic has remained silent on any similar initiatives.

item.title: Trump, congressional Republicans float suspending federal gas tax amid Iran war
item.description: Voters are souring on Trump's economy ahead of the 2026 midterm elections, and high gas prices are only adding to consumer discontent.

item.title: Indian Ocean more contested, and important than ever before: Vice Admiral Swaminathan
item.description: He observed that the Indian Ocean has always been India’s arena, and highlighted the growing significance of the region

item.title: India’s new FTAs offer investment opportunities, but utilisation remains weak: Commerce Secretary
item.description: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal urged industries to leverage India's newly finalised free trade agreements, noting a historical underutilization of such pacts. While FTAs have boosted trade volumes, exports have lagged behind imports, potentially due to strong domestic demand in India's rapidly growing economy. New agreements with developed nations are expected to attract investment by going beyond tariffs.

item.title: India plans ‘Made in India’ brand scheme, targets gaps in domestic manufacturing
item.description: India is identifying 100 products for enhanced domestic manufacturing, including auto parts, to boost production for global markets. A 'Made in India Brand Scheme' is planned, following a successful pilot in the steel sector, to assure quality and value addition. Companies are urged to invest long-term, leveraging opportunities from new free trade agreements and the impact of AI on manufacturing.

item.title: Ramaphosa Shuns Calls to Quit as Lawmakers Ready Probe
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would contest a report that criticized his handling of a robbery at his farm in 2020 after a ruling by the nation’s top court resurrected the scandal, and rejected calls to stand down.

item.title: PM Modi Urges Schools To Consider Online Classes To Cut Fuel Use
item.description: Online learning became common across India and much of the world during the Covid-19 pandemic, when classrooms shifted into homes during lockdowns.

item.title: TeamPCP Compromises Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Weeks After KICS Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has confirmed that a modified version of the Jenkins AST plugin was published to the Jenkins Marketplace. "If you are using Checkmarx Jenkins AST plugin, you need to ensure that you are using the version 2.0.13-829.vc72453fa_1c16 that was published on December 17, 2025 or previously," the cybersecurity company said in a statement over the weekend. As of writing, Checkmarx has released

item.title: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants
item.description: The painting is believed to have been plundered by high-ranking Nazi Hermann Goering during World War Two.

item.title: UK sanctions network accused of planning attacks for Iran
item.description: Britain freezes assets and travel for those accused of laundering billions and plotting Iranian-backed hostile activity.

item.title: Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over role in Duterte’s drug war
item.description: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has evaded a drug war warrant against him.

item.title: Global Energy Shock From Iran War May Extend Till 2027: Saudi Aramco Chief
item.description: Crude prices jumped during the first quarter from the mid $60s in early February to over $100 a barrel.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
item.description: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

item.title: Drug gang attacks ‘force hundreds of Indigenous families to flee’ in Mexico
item.description: Violence in Guerrero state has driven as many as 1,000 households from their homes, rights group says Hundreds of Indigenous families have been forced to flee their homes in the mountains of central Mexico by intense attacks from a local criminal group, including drone bombings, an Indigenous rights organisation said on Monday . A gang known as Los Ardillos has been carrying out attacks in Guerrero state for years, b

item.title: Google warns hackers are using AI to build zero-day exploit for planned mass cyberattack - Crypto Briefing
item.description: Google warns hackers are using AI to build zero-day exploit for planned mass cyberattack Crypto Briefing

item.title: Google announces its first-ever discovery of a zero-day exploit made with AI
item.description: The Google Threat Intelligence Group said its proactive measures stopped a "mass exploitation event."

item.title: Google says it likely thwarted effort by hacker group to use AI for 'mass exploitation event'
item.description: Hackers are rapidly adopting AI to find previously unknown software flaws even without the help of Anthropic's powerful Mythos model.

item.title: Trump after rejecting Iran’s proposal, says ceasefire at its ‘weakest’ point and on ‘massive life support’
item.description: Speaking at the Oval Office, Trump sharply criticized Iran’s response to ongoing negotiations, calling the proposal a “piece of garbage.” Trump doubled down on his criticism and said he did not even finish reading the document.

item.title: India-US trade talks set for next round, but Section 301 probe remains hurdle
item.description: India anticipates a US trade delegation for upcoming talks, which will address all outstanding issues, including the Section 301 probe. A proposed trade deal with the US will not progress until this investigation concludes. The US initiated the Section 301 investigation in March, alleging India's excess capacity in several sectors.

item.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Under Active Exploitation to Deploy Filemanager Backdoor
item.description: A threat actor named Mr_Rot13 has been attributed to the exploitation of a recently disclosed critical cPanel flaw to deploy a backdoor codenamed Filemanager on compromised environments. The attack exploits CVE-2026-41940, a vulnerability impacting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) that could result in an authentication bypass and allow remote attackers to gain elevated control of the control

item.title: Trump says Iran’s response to peace proposal ‘unacceptable’
item.description: Oil prices climb again as Washington and Tehran fail to agree terms to end war and reopen Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Starbucks to cut 61 technology jobs in Seattle
item.description: Starbucks is making headlines with the announcement of 61 tech job reductions at its Seattle HQ, signaling a pivotal phase in the company’s transformation journey. These layoffs align with an overarching strategy to sharpen focus on elevating customer experiences and driving revenue growth. This decision comes after prior restructuring efforts, including the closure of some locations.

item.title: Redistricting war isn't over, Democrats say as Jeffries calls a meeting this week
item.description: Democrats are vowing to fight on after being dealt a series of blows in the partisan fight to redraw congressional maps ahead of the 2026 midterms.

item.title: Will hear Trinamool pleas on victory margins being less than deleted votes in SIR, says Supreme Court
item.description: This is the first Supreme Court hearing in the West Bengal SIR case after the election; in as many as 31 seats, the victory margins of the BJP were less than the number of votes deleted during the SIR, Trinamool leaders say

item.title: Trump Plans US Gas Tax Cut As Iran War Spurs Price Surge
item.description: Suspending the federal fuel tax would bring those prices down by about four per cent.

item.title: Tragedy at sea: MV Hondius captain praises unity amid hantavirus outbreak.
item.description: For unity and strength, a thank you message from the captain of hantavirus-stricken ship.

item.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. A few months before he was awarded the Nobel Prize in economics in 2024, Daron Acemoglu published a paper that earned him few fans in Silicon Valley. Contrary to what Big Tech…
